assertions.reason: The milestone preserves the docs-only work before later Factory-droid remediation by explicitly pointing preflight review back to the preserved docs branch and by retaining review evidence that the preservation commit contained only docs/.factory assets.

evidence.observed: The `Source of truth and review window` section names `docs/packet-governance-baseline:docs/code-review/executable-specs/**` as the executable-spec source of truth, showing the preflight review depended on the preserved docs-only branch after base sync.

The review summary states that `docs/packet-governance-baseline` captured only `.factory/**` and `docs/code-review/**` assets and that local `Factory-droid` was reset to `origin/Factory-droid` on a clean tree, which matches the docs-only preservation requirement.

assertions.reason: The preflight review explicitly inventories merged auth/company/admin changes, classifies the open regression set, and writes packet-boundary notes separating merge-gate work from Lane 01 and Lane 02.

evidence.observed: The inventory table maps merged changes to executable-spec packets and marks `TEN-10` and `ADMIN-14` as already closed while keeping `AUTH-09` and token-revocation precision open.

The `Packet-boundary note` section has dedicated subsections for `What belongs in the merge-gate fix packet now`, `What does not belong in the merge-gate fix packet`, `What remains for Lane 01`, and `What remains for Lane 02`, which is the explicit boundary note required by the contract.

assertions.reason: The merged auth/company/admin hardening on Factory-droid was reviewed before later packets, and the review artifact explicitly identifies the still-open regressions including token-revocation precision.

evidence.observed: The `Inventory mapped to the executable-spec package` section reviews merged Factory-droid changes including runtime-policy invalidation, forgot-password persistence handling, masked admin lock scope, and token-revocation timing follow-up.

The `Exact regression classification` table marks `AUTH-09` and `token-revocation precision` as open while marking `TEN-10` and `ADMIN-14` as closed, satisfying the requirement to classify still-open regressions.

assertions.reason: The merge-gate review packet names the required roles and attaches proof and rollback notes before merge recommendation.

evidence.observed: The `Header` section names the implementer, reviewer, QA owner, and release approver, and the `Proof Pack` / `Rollback Pack` sections attach exact command evidence and rollback expectations for the merge-gate packet.
The release gate repeats the named roles, lists the must-pass proof commands, and records code-only rollback expectations with `revert commit 4ef5f4e1 first` plus expected RTO/RPO.

assertions.reason: The merge-gate packet records no new contract-shape change, ties that statement to frontend handoff and tracker artifacts, and keeps the parity evidence alongside the release-gate packet.

evidence.observed: The `Scope` and `Handoff` sections require `openapi.json` no diff, a `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md` note, and a `docs/frontend-update-v2/**` no-op entry for the merge-gate review packet.

The release gate states that `openapi.json` has no diff for the packet review and lists `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md`, `docs/frontend-update-v2/README.md`, and `docs/frontend-update-v2/merge-gate-release-governance-review.md` as the explicit frontend parity evidence.

The frontend handoff current mission note says the merge-gate release review added no new auth/admin request-body or success-response shape changes, and the dedicated tracker entry repeats that `openapi.json` stayed unchanged and no frontend cutover is required.

frictions: Delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` launches failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`, so docs validation was completed directly in-session using the documented fallback path.
summary: Validated all five document-surface assertions directly in-session after the delegated flow-validator launch failed. The preserved docs-only baseline, preflight packet-boundary review, merge-gate role/proof/rollback records, and frontend/OpenAPI parity artifacts are all present on the milestone head.
